summary: Policy-backed control for tile notifications. The local Microsoft WPN.admx file explicitly documents the same user policy key, value name, and enabled/disabled values that the app uses.

setting.name: Tile Notifications
setting.casual_explanation: Tile notifications are the live updates and badges some Windows tiles can show. This policy decides whether those tile updates are allowed for the current user.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable tile notifications to reduce motion and noise in Start or because they do not want apps surfacing live tile updates.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Turning this off makes Start quieter, but apps and system features will not be able to update their tiles and tile badges.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft documents DisallowTileNotification in the Notifications Policy CSP. The app uses the user-side policy path HKCU\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\PushNotifications\NoTileApplicationNotification = 1, which matches the documented disable behavior.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: Tile notifications stay enabled by default and can still be controlled normally.

allowed_values.label: Tile notifications allowed
allowed_values.meaning: The policy is disabled and applications can still update tiles and tile badges.

allowed_values.label: Tile notifications turned off
allowed_values.meaning: Applications and system features cannot update tiles and tile badges for the current user.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes NoTileApplicationNotification = 1 for the current user, which matches the documented disable behavior.

windows_defaults.label: Windows user-policy default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Supported Windows editions with tile notification policy support

states.rationale: The user policy is not configured by default.

windows_defaults.notes: Microsoft documents the functional default as tile notifications enabled when the policy is not configured.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: People who still use live tile updates
avoid_for: Users who want a quieter Start surface
recommended_profiles.good_when: You want tiles to keep updating normally.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want to stop live tile updates and badges.
tradeoffs: Tiles can continue surfacing changing content.

recommended_profiles.label: Quiet Start
intended_for: Users who do not want live tiles updating
avoid_for: Users who depend on tile badges or live tile content

states.rationale: Block tile notifications and tile badge updates.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Start to feel calmer and more static.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You use live tiles as an information source.
tradeoffs: Apps and system features lose tile and badge updates.

evidence.title: Microsoft Policy CSP: Notifications / DisallowTileNotification
evidence.summary: Official Microsoft documentation for the policy that turns off tile notifications.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft WPN.admx NoTileNotification mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file maps NoTileNotification to the PushNotifications policy key with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft WPN.adml NoTileNotification help text
evidence.summary: The local ADML help text says enabling the policy turns off tile notifications and prevents applications and system features from updating tiles and tile badges.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes NoTileApplicationNotification = 1 for the current tweak.

decision.why: The local Microsoft WPN.admx file explicitly defines NoTileNotification at SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\PushNotifications with valueName NoTileApplicationNotification and enabledValue 1 / disabledValue 0, which matches the app's user policy surface.

validation_proof.notes: The local official Microsoft ADMX file names the same user policy key and value name that the app writes and confirms the 1/0 mapping.
